title: Importance attribution via Shapley / Johnson's ε
summary: Rust port of `relaimpo::calc.relimp(type="lmg")` (exact Shapley), `iopsych::relWt` (Johnson's ε), plus Castro–Gómez–Tejada sampled Shapley for medium-p. One CSV in → one ε per variable out, summing to R² of the full model (minus the `always` block's R², if any).
description: Reads a CSV, auto-detects numeric vs categorical columns (one-hot expands factors, dropping the alphabetically-first level as the reference), centres + standardises as configured, and computes weighted covariance moments. Variables — not expanded dummies — are the unit of attribution: a factor with K levels is a single group of K-1 columns and gets a single ε. Exact Shapley enumerates all 2^p subsets of free groups and averages marginal R² contributions with Shapley weights; sampled Shapley draws random orderings and averages telescoping prefix marginals (reports a standard error); Johnson's ε eigendecomposes the predictor correlation matrix, projects onto the best-fitting orthogonal approximation, and sums squared projection loadings. `method=auto` picks exact if free groups ≤ shapley_max_p, sampled if ≤ shapley_sampled_max_p, Johnson otherwise. An `always` block is partialled out first via a block-inverse identity, and the attribution decomposes R²_full − R²_always across the free groups. Outputs: `importance.csv` (variable, eps, eps_rel, rank, [eps_se], is_always) and a multi-sheet XLSX (summary, importance, coefficients, factor_levels).

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: CSV of predictors + response. Non-numeric columns are auto-detected as factors and one-hot expanded.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run; no '..', '/', or '\\'.

arguments.name: vars
arguments.description: Predictor subset. Empty = all non-y, non-always, non-weights columns.

arguments.name: always
arguments.description: Predictors whose R² is attributed as a single block (not decomposed). Matches `relaimpo::calc.relimp(always=...)`.

arguments.name: weights_col
arguments.description: Optional per-observation weights column. Missing = unit weights.

arguments.name: shapley_max_p
arguments.description: Exact-Shapley upper bound on free groups. Hard-capped at 20 (2^20 subsets).

arguments.name: shapley_sampled_max_p
arguments.description: Sampled-Shapley upper bound on free groups; above this `auto` falls back to Johnson.

arguments.name: n_orderings
arguments.description: Random orderings for sampled Shapley. SE shrinks as 1/√N.

arguments.name: seed
arguments.description: RNG seed for sampled Shapley (deterministic ChaCha20).

arguments.name: standardize
arguments.description: Standardise predictors + response. R²/ε invariant; only β reporting is affected.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether CSV inputs have a header row.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of worker threads. `1` (default) runs serially. Parallelises exact-Shapley subset enumeration (2^p R² evaluations) and sampled-Shapley orderings. Results are bit-deterministic across thread counts.

notes: `ε_j` sums to R²_full across free groups (Shapley) or to R²_full (Johnson, when `always` is empty). With an `always` block present, free ε sums to R²_full − R²_always.
Factors expand to `var=level` columns internally; the importance table reports a single row per original variable.
`auto` resolves once per fit: if free groups ≤ shapley_max_p → exact Shapley; ≤ shapley_sampled_max_p → sampled Shapley; else Johnson.
Sampled Shapley returns a standard error per variable. Exact Shapley and Johnson do not (the uncertainty is purely algorithmic, not sampling).
Exact Shapley over 20 free groups is refused hard (2^20 subsets). Use `shapley_sampled` or `johnson` for larger group counts.

artifacts.title: Variable importance table
artifacts.description: The primary output. One row per predictor variable with its Shapley or Johnson importance score — the share of the model's R² attributed to that variable. Scores sum to the total R² of the full model, so they can be read as percentages. Use this to tell clients which drivers matter most and by how much.

fields.name: epsilon
fields.description: Importance score: share of total R² attributed to this variable.

fields.name: epsilon_pct
fields.description: Importance as a percentage of total attributed R².

fields.name: se
fields.description: Standard error (sampled Shapley only; blank for exact Shapley and Johnson).

artifacts.title: Model summary
artifacts.description: JSON record of the method used (exact Shapley, sampled, or Johnson), total R², always-block R², seed, and run metadata.
